entry.title: Understanding field(suppress) in SAP RAP and How It Differs from @UI.hidden and @Consumption.hidden
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

When working with SAP RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model), the Behavior Definition (BDEF) offers several keywords to control how data is exposed and handled. One of the less-discussed but powerful ones is field(suppress).

This blog is not just about syntax — it’s about what actually happens at runtime, the edge cases I hit, and why I chose suppression over readonly.

Requirement: In had one requirement, customer service reps needed to update Priority, Deadline Date, Cancel Reason, but they should never touch Created Time or Created User. These audit fields must remain intact, even during draft saves or mass updates.

What I Tried First
@UI.hidden: Hid the fields in Fiori, but they were still exposed in OData payloads.

field(readonly): Made the fields immutable, but they still appeared in EML structures — meaning a payload could attempt to overwrite them.

Both approaches failed to fully protect the audit fields.

What is field(suppress)?
field(suppress) is a static feature control in the BDEF that removes a field from RAP's transactional behavior layer. When a field is marked as suppressed, RAP excludes it from:

  • EML (Entity Manipulation Language) structures — such as TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE, TYPE STRUCTURE FOR UPDATE
  • RAP-generated behavior APIs
  • Behavior-related metadata

Runtime Behavior of Suppressed Fields
When I switched to field(suppress), here’s what I observed:

In EML statements, suppressed fields simply don’t exist. If you try to reference them, RAP silently ignores them, no runtime dump, no short dump.

If you mistakenly add them in the mapping section, RAP throws a framework error at activation time.

In draft handling, suppressed fields are still maintained internally, but you can’t overwrite them. This was critical for audit consistency.

In determinations, you cannot reference suppressed fields — the compiler blocks it. I observed this when trying to auto-populate Created By and Created At.

Syntax:

define behavior for ZI_REQHDR {
    field ( suppress ) CreatedAt, CreatedBy; 
}

Attempting to map them in the behavior pool raises:
Field "CreatedBy" cannot be suppressed.

Key insight: field(suppress) is a behavior-layer protection tool — it removes a field from RAP-generated transactional types and APIs. It is not a UI visibility control or an OData filter

Why Suppress Instead of Readonly
Readonly: Field is visible but immutable — still exposed in payloads.

Suppress: Field is completely removed from RAP’s transactional model — safest option for audit fields.

Note: Key fields (primary key fields) cannot be suppressed.

  • Suppressed fields must also be excluded from the field mapping. Attempting to include them raises a framework error:
mapping for zdt_vd_reqhdr corresponding
{
    RequestUuid   = request_uuid;
    Status        = status;
    Priority      = priority;
    ExternalId    = external_id;
    DeadlineDate  = deadline_date;
    RequesterId   = requester_id;
    CancelReason  = cancel_reason;

* // If i try to add the below fields error will get raised
*   CreatedBy     = created_by; // Field "CreatedBy" cannot suppressed
*   CreatedAt     = created_at; // Field "CreatedAt" cannot suppressed

    LastChangedBy = last_changed_by;
    LastChangedAt = last_changed_at;
}

Behavior Pool: Effect on EML Strutcure

  • Once suppressed, the fields are no longer available in EML operations. For example, in a MODIFY ENTITIES call, suppressed fields do not appear in the FIELDS ( ) selector:
 MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_vd_reqhdr IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY RequestHeader
    UPDATE FIELDS (  ) 
* i try add the fields which i have done suppressed those not visible here 
    WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_key IN keys
                  ( %key = ls_key-%key
                  Status = 100 ) )
    REPORTED DATA(lt_report).

  • This confirms that suppressed fields are cleanly removed from the generated TYPE STRUCTURE FOR UPDATE and equivalent EML types.

How Does It Differ from @ui.hidden and @Consumption.hidden?

All three mechanisms can "hide" a field, but they operate at different layers:

Mechanism Layer Effect
field(suppress)RAP behavior / EMLRemoves field from RAP transactional APIs and EML structures
@ui.hidden: trueUI renderingHides the field from generated Fiori screens; field may still appear in OData metadata and API payloads
@Consumption.hidden: trueOData consumptionHides the field from OData consumers and Fiori Elements scenarios
  • Use field(suppress) to protect a field from the RAP behavior layer entirely.
  • Use @ui.hidden to keep a field out of the UI while it remains accessible in APIs.
  • Use @Consumption.hidden to hide a field from external OData consumers.

Conclusion

field(suppress), @ui.hidden, and @Consumption.hidden serve distinct purposes in the RAP stack and should not be treated as interchangeable. When you have internal technical fields — timestamps, UUIDs, control fields — that RAP should never expose through its transactional model, field(suppress) is the right tool. Pair it with @Consumption.hidden to lock down the field at both the behavior and OData layers.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Public Edition, Bank Communication Management
entry.content:
Hi Treasury,

Short answer: The Rule IDs in the matchcode are SAP-delivered standard BCM grouping rules — they are not customer-maintainable in Public Cloud, and the rule that lands on the batch is determined automatically by the grouping engine (by payment method + priority), not forced by the assignment you made in the "Assign Approval Rules for Bank Communication Management" activity.

Where are the rules in the Rule ID matchcode (6_PM_&, 6_PM_0 … 6_PM_6) maintained?

They are not maintained by you — they are delivered by SAP.

In Public Cloud there is no customer activity equivalent to the on-premise FSCM → BCM → Payment Grouping → Rule Maintenance step, which is where these rules would otherwise be created. That activity is not exposed under clean core — which is exactly why you can't find the maintenance step.

This is consistent with how SAP handles comparable BCM-internal rules in Public Cloud: they are pre-defined and hardcoded and cannot be changed by the user. Reference (analogous principle, posting rules): KBA 3374309.

The activity you are in — Assign Approval Rules for Bank Communication Management — is the Public Cloud counterpart of the APM on-premise step Define Rules for Bank Communication Management (FSCM → Advanced Payment Management → Payment Processing → External Interfaces → BCM). Its job is only to map an existing BCM Rule ID to a key combination (order type / company code / house bank / account ID) for the APM→BCM workflow handover — not to create the rule. Reference: SAP S/4HANA APM + MBC blog.

Summary

The matchcode rules are SAP-delivered and cannot be maintained in Public Cloud, so there is no missing step to find. The batch correctly shows the delivered grouping rule that matches its payment method by priority, not the one you assigned. Verify the payment method → rule/priority match, and if you need the determination logic confirmed or a specific rule applied, raise an SAP CASE since the rules are not in your control.

Hope it helps,

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: SAP Developer News for June 25th, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Upcoming Events

  • We are Developers
  • CodeConnect

SAP Open Source Webinar on the NeoNephos Foundation

  • Webinar details https://events.sap.com/ospo-webinar-series/en_us/home.html

ABAPConf  Recordings

  • YouTube Recordings

DYK GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation

  • More information: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/create-sap-build-apps-applications-in-minutes-with-ai/ba-p/14054862

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Upcoming Events

1:40 SAP Open Source Webinar on the NeoNephos Foundation

2:38 ABAPConf Recordings

3:45 DYK GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation

Transcription

 

00;00;00;00 - 00;00;06;00
Announcer
This is the SAP Developer News for June the 25th, 2026.

00;00;06;02 - 00;00;09;24
Thomas Jung
the Developer Advocates are always on the move and evidently almost never

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;26;23
Thomas Jung
at home. Over the next couple of weeks, besides our normal schedule of CodeJam, we got a couple of special events coming up and just a little over a week many of us are going to be at. We are Developers in Berlin. Now, this isn’t an SAP.

00;00;26;25 - 00;00;53;24
Thomas Jung
This is not a specific SAP event, but one just a general event for developers from all around the world. I'll be there. Witalij and Shilpa and Sheena will also be there. So if you happen to find yourself at this event, stop by, say hi, maybe attend one of our sessions. I'm giving a session on Friday about how AI is affecting Developer Relations, Developer Advocacy.

00;00;53;24 - 00;01;25;04
Thomas Jung
And maybe I'll tell you my story about how an AI coding agent decided it was its role to lie to me. It should be a fun story. But then the week after that, many of us will be at CodeConnect and near the SAP main campus. Over in St Leon/ROT, we have HANACon, UI5Con and reCAP, as well as some CodeJams that week, and a great many of the Developer Advocates will be in town to support those events.

00;01;25;06 - 00;01;41;18
Thomas Jung
Speak at them, run the CodeJams, so we look forward to seeing many of you at CodeConnect. So busy couple of weeks as we kick off the summertime in the Northern Hemisphere here, and we hope to see you at an event soon.

00;01;41;21 - 00;02;15;21
DJ Adams
Coming up next month on Friday the 10th of July, there's another SAP Open Source webinar. It's open to all. And the topic is the Neo Foundation Europe's Cloud Sovereignty in Motion. The foundation was established in 2025 as a Linux Foundation Europe initiative, and its core objectives are to strengthen European tech sovereignty, improve interoperability standards, and to enable integrated cloud to edge solutions.

00;02;15;23 - 00;02;34;25
DJ Adams
In this webinar hosted by SAP's Open source Program Office, you can learn how the foundation has evolved, how it's governed, and what it offers, a link to more details, and how and where to register is in the description.

00;02;34;27 - 00;03;06;05
Thomas Jung
This week we saw the ABAPConf online and in-person hybrid event take place, and if you miss it, there are plenty of recordings available on YouTube. In fact, both live streams from the whole day. Track one. Track two are available on YouTube, so something like nearly 18 hours worth of content 18 hours plus we saw some great sessions there by some of our ABAP PM and development colleagues.

00;03;06;08 - 00;03;37;21
Thomas Jung
You know, kicking off the day, talking about the future of ABAP Cloud, about the RAP development model, about ABAP Development Tools now available in Visual Studio Code, and what we're doing with AI tooling there. And at the end of the day, we saw a special presentation by Karl Kessler as he looked back at the history of ABAP. And so all that great content is available on the ABAPConf page on YouTube.

00;03;37;21 - 00;03;46;22
Thomas Jung
And if you're interested in ABAP definitely suggest you check it out.

00;03;46;25 - 00;04;18;06
Rekha D R
Did you know you can enable and use generative AI feature with an SAP Build Process Automation? For that, click on Automated Process Project, provide the name and create the project. Then create a process within it. Switch to the overview page, then click on generate button. Now you have the options to create process, form and data type. When you open the process and click generate button, you can explain this process.

00;04;18;09 - 00;04;36;06
Rekha D R
You can create a business form. For example, a sales order form and AI will intelligently generate the entire form with relevant input fields within the decision artifact. You can summarize the decision. Also have the option to generate new rules with your natural language.

entry.title: Re: Notification only if all IDOCs in a day fails
entry.content:
Hi, Deepika,

I am not sure if I understand your exact requirement.  I think you are saying for a given sender, only if ALL Idocs for that sender fail, then alert?  If so, I don't know that this is possible.

KBA 3597393 -  Cloud ALM Integration & Exception monitoring - Event Filters and Data Collection filters for idocs and Help doc: SAP S/4HANA & SAP Business Suite 7 have information that details the configuration for Event filters and data collection filters in Cloud ALM's Integration & Exception Monitoring.  Hopefully this helps clarify what you are trying to configure.

In case you want a feature that is not yet available, you can log your request (check for existing entries already logged and up-vote them) in the Influence Campaign:

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation   https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Operations           https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Service                 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3669
    Best regards,
    Paula

entry.title: Re: Request for Metrics and Threshold Details – SAP Cloud...
entry.content:
Hi 1-Jul,
I appears that part of your question is not available, so I am not sure of the full intent of it.
In the SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring app, metrics can be :

  • Numeric KPIs that evaluate against warning/critical thresholds (for example, memory utilization, CPU, queue lengths).
  • Up/Down or Reachable/Not Reachable status of services or components.
  • Discrete occurrences (for example, certificate expiring soon, failed checks, or system-generated alerts).
  • Events can also be custom-defined.

Recommended threshold values

  • SAP ships default thresholds for many metrics. These defaults are visible in the Health Monitoring app for each managed service/system and can be customized.
  • “Recommended” values depend on system size, workload pattern, and solution type (for example, SAP HANA, ABAP platform, Integration Suite, etc.). SAP’s default thresholds serve as the baseline and are the recommended starting point.

How to locate and share the exact metric lists and defaults

  • In SAP Cloud ALM, built-in help has a wealth of information:
    • Go to Health Monitoring
    • Open the specific managed service/system
    • Navigate to Metrics
    • Each metric shows its current default threshold (warning/critical) and type along with a description
      See also KBA 3613788 - Where to find documentation on what Ok, Warning, Critical and Fatal means in regards to percentages within Health Monitoring? - Cloud ALM
  • On Help Portal:
    • Health Monitoring - Content -> breaks down by Product the various metric categories and metrics
    • To find what metrics are available in a use case: 
      1. The SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert Portal has sections for each Operations use case (e.g. Health Monitoring).
      2. Access the "Content" page for a use case.
      3. The supported metrics on a per-product basis will be listed on the page.
      4. Some pages have a filterable table, where you can type the name of the specific SAP Product you are interested and see the supported metrics for only that product. 

Hopefully the above info is helpful in answering your question.
Best regards,
Paula
